sanskrit:
सत्त्वं रजस्तम इति गुणाः प्रकृतिसम्भवाः।
निबध्नन्ति महाबाहो देहे देहिनमव्ययम्॥
transliteration:
sattvaṃ rajastama iti guṇāḥ prakṛtisambhavāḥ
nibadhnanti mahābāho dehe dehinam avyayam
literal_meaning: The three gunas—sattva (clarity), rajas (activity), tamas (inertia)—born of material nature, bind the eternal embodied one within the body.
contextual_meaning: Our personality is shaped by three interacting neuro-affective programs that automatically color perception, motivation and mood. Recognizing them is the first step toward conscious self-regulation.
psychological_state: Meta-cognitive awakening—realizing that moods and traits are not 'me' but temporary patterns to observe.
trigger_scenario: You snap at your partner, then wonder 'Why did I lose it?'—recognizing the rajas surge and learning to pause before reacting.
anchor_text: Notice which inner mood—clear, restless, or heavy—is running you right now.
